Name: Yoroniku (YORONIKU)
Known For: Silk Rose (thin-sliced wagyu wrapped around rice), Truffle Sukiyaki
Address: 6-6-22 Minami-Aoyama, Luna Rossa B1F, Minato-ku, Tokyo
Reservations: Yes - book via TableCheck or OMAKASE; auto-cancelled if 15min late
Notes: Tabelog Top 100 2025 (score 4.16). Birthplace of wagyu wrapped in bite-sized rice ball. Hidden in backstreets of Minami Aoyama. Truffle Sukiyaki is the must-order. Also has branches in Ebisu and Azabudai Hills. Multiple sources: Reddit, Tabelog, TripAdvisor, SAVOR JAPAN.

Name: SATO Briand Honten (SATOburian)
Known For: Chateaubriand from Kobe/Kyushu beef, Straight Ball Course (8 cuts)
Reservations: Yes - extremely hard; slots open 25th of each month for following month
Notes: 8 consecutive years on Tabelog Top 100. Tabelog Gold 2017, Bronze 2025. Official Kobe Beef designated restaurant since 2023. Called one of Japan's toughest reservations. Multiple sources: Tabelog, Reddit, food blogs.

Name: Yakiniku Jumbo Hanare
Reservations: Yes - mandatory; expect 1-2 month wait; book via TableCheck
Notes: Tabelog Silver Award 2025, score 4.33. TripAdvisor 4.7/5. One of only 5-6 yakiniku restaurants in Tokyo that respect beef at the highest level. Exclusively A5 Kuroge wagyu. Multiple sources: Tabelog, TripAdvisor, Reddit.

Name: Nikutei Futago iki Shinjuku
Known For: 80-dish wagyu menu with Ota beef (Hyogo) and Sendai beef (Miyagi)
Notes: 10th-floor Kabukicho location. Meat Box (5,000 yen) is popular starting point. Reviewers call it the best wagyu BBQ experience. Good for groups. 6min from Shinjuku Station East Exit. Multiple sources: TripAdvisor, byfood.

Name: Uokin Honten
Known For: 6-item sashimi platter, fresh seafood at wholesale prices
Address: Dai-2 Fuji Bldg B1-3F, 3-18-3 Shimbashi, Minato-ku, Tokyo
Reservations: No - walk-in; expect queue after 6pm weekdays
Notes: Classic salaryman izakaya. Owned by fish wholesale company so prices are unbeatable. Signature 6-item sashimi platter is a must. Counter seating, noisy, smokers -- authentic charm. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, TimeOut, Tabelog.

Name: Kanemasu
Known For: Uniku (beef roll with sea urchin), standing izakaya with Tsukiji-fresh seafood
Reservations: No - walk-in only; GO EARLY, best sellers sell out 30min after opening
Notes: Famous standing izakaya near former Tsukiji. Uniku (uni + beef roll, 3,000 yen) is the signature dish. Was packed at 3:30pm on a Tuesday. TimeOut Tokyo calls it one of best izakayas. Multiple sources: TimeOut, Tabelog, Reddit.

Name: Ginza Shimada
Known For: Kaiseki-quality standing bar, seasonal dishes at fraction of fine-dining prices
Notes: Tabelog Top 100 Tachinomi 2025 (score 3.66). Chef formerly at 3-Michelin-star Azabu Yukimura. Dishes start at 500 yen. Chilled soba with karasumi or uni with spiny lobster are standouts. Pioneered high-end tachinomi trend. Multiple sources: PUNCH, Tabelog, TimeOut.

Name: Birdland (Ginza)
Known For: Michelin-starred yakitori, Okukuji Shamo chicken, legendary liver pate
Address: Ginza 4-2-15, Tsukamoto Sozan Bldg B1F, Chuo-ku, Tokyo
Notes: Michelin 1-star since 2010. Chef Wada is a yakitori legend. Only 16 counter seats. Liver pate and wasabi-coated rare sasami are must-orders. Juicy yet has bounce to the bite. Closed Sun-Mon. Multiple sources: Michelin, Tabelog, Reddit, TripAdvisor.

Name: Toriki (Kinshicho)
Known For: World's first Michelin-starred yakitori, Akadori chicken over binchotan
Reservations: Recommended - weekends book up; try weekday after 9pm
Notes: First yakitori in the world to earn Michelin star (2010), held for 10 years. Counter-only. Chef grills at incredibly high temps, fanning with uchiwa fan. Tsukune and liver are standouts. Mon-Sat. Multiple sources: Michelin, Tabelog, TimeOut, Reddit.

Name: Hakushu (Kobe Teppanyaki)
Known For: 50+ year heritage Kobe beef teppanyaki, intimate counter
Address: 17-10 Sakuragaokacho, MCD Bldg B1F, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo
Notes: Founded 1966, over 50 years in Shibuya. TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice 6 consecutive years, 4.7/5, #19 of 5,404 Shibuya restaurants. Called best hidden gem and best teppanyaki experience. Intimate basement. Multiple sources: TripAdvisor, Tabelog, Reddit, social media.

Name: Bifuteki Kawamura Ginza
Known For: Authentic Kobe Beef pioneer in Kanto, luxurious private rooms
Address: Ginza Museum Bldg 10-11F, 6-5-6 Ginza, Chuo-ku, Tokyo
Reservations: Yes - via web form or call +81-50-2030-4678
Notes: Founded 1972, credited with bringing authentic Kobe Beef to Kanto. Relocated/reopened Ginza branch 2025 with private rooms. Ribeye 100g from 16,500 yen, Filet 100g from 19,800 yen. English menu and staff. 2min from Ginza Station. Multiple sources: SAVOR JAPAN, Tabelog, TripAdvisor.

Name: Ginkuma Saryo
Known For: Kuroge Wagyu & A5 Sendai beef, seasonal kaiseki-style teppanyaki courses
Address: 7F Nishi Ginza Bldg, 6-3-11 Ginza, Chuo-ku, Tokyo
Notes: Warm cozy teppanyaki in Ginza. Special Wagyu Course 10,000 yen for 7 dishes -- strong Ginza value. Seasonal kaiseki elements. Open lunch and dinner. 3min from Ginza Station C2/C3. Multiple sources: SAVOR JAPAN, Tabelog, Pelago.

Name: Nikuya Tanaka Ginza
Known For: Omakase wagyu kaiseki by meat master Satoru Tanaka
Address: 9F GICROS GINZA GEMS, 6-4-3 Ginza, Chuo-ku, Tokyo
Reservations: Yes - required well in advance; only 9 seats, 2 seatings (5pm & 8:30pm)
Notes: Chef Satoru Tanaka is revered meat expert. Single omakase at 48,000 yen + 10% service. Most exclusive wagyu cuts including Kobe and Matsusaka. Only 9 seats. Smart casual dress code. Closed Sundays. For special occasions. Multiple sources: Tabelog, TABLEALL, Pocket Concierge.
